candidates.notes: Absorption detected with bimodal distribution (7 HIGH, 19 LOW). Main feature activation is lower for HIGH absorption features (1164 vs 1376, r=-0.285), suggesting absorbed features are less active. However, direct steering had minimal effect due to weak first-letter features in GPT-2 Small and steering bypassing encoder. Reconstruction MSE is extremely high and cosine similarity negative, indicating poor SAE reconstruction quality.

pilot_absorption.key_finding: 7/26 features show HIGH absorption (>50%), 19/26 show LOW (<10%). Mean absorption rate 27%. Feature identification has quality issues (6 letters map to same feature 25906).

pilot_steering.key_finding: Main feature activation lower for HIGH absorption features (1164 vs 1376). Direct steering minimal due to weak GPT-2 features. Reconstruction fidelity shows detectable differences between HIGH and LOW absorption.

issues: Feature identification: 6 letters (A,U,V,W,X,Y,Z) map to same feature 25906
Bimodal distribution (0% or 100%) suggests threshold sensitivity
GPT-2 Small may not exhibit strong absorption per evolution lessons
SAE reconstruction quality is poor (negative cosine similarity, very high MSE)
Steering bypasses encoder, making it robust to encoder-side absorption (known confound)
Small sample size (n=26) limits statistical power for correlation analysis
recommendations: Use contrastive activation patching for better feature identification
Test on larger model (Pythia-160M or Gemma-2-2B)
Add semantic hierarchy features in addition to first-letter
Include multiple layers for cross-layer validation
Focus on activation-based metrics (reconstruction fidelity, feature activation strength) rather than steering
Add power analysis - current n=26 may be underpowered for small effects
